title: STAT+: How a biotech turned a trial failure into an AI model
summary: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: How a biotech startup turned a failed clinical trial into an AI model, and more.

title: Lilly buys non-opioid pain drugmaker 4E
summary: It’s the latest in a string of deals for Lilly, which has sunk more than $18 billion into acquisitions over the past few years.

title: Express Scripts, PCMA sue to block Tennesee law breaking up PBMs and pharmacies
summary: Express Scripts and the PBM lobby are following in CVS Caremark’s footsteps in filing complaints challenging the FAIR Rx Act, which was passed earlier this year despite vehement opposition from PBMs.

title: FDA staff scrutinizes evidence supporting Moderna’s flu vaccine
summary: Ahead of a Thursday advisory panel meeting, agency scientists highlighted shortcomings in data accrued for a vaccine the FDA controversially refused to even review earlier this year.

title: The U.S. infant mortality rate fell to an all-time low, though it still trails other similar nations
summary: Infant mortality in the U.S. dropped to a new all-time low in 2025, according to CDC data, though it still trails other similar nations.

title: STAT+: ARCH-launched Neumora stops depression program
summary: A new AI model to improve trial enrollment, importing drugs from Canada, and more biotech news from The Readout

title: Edgewise heart drug passes key trial test
summary: Still, the results leave somewhat unclear how different the company’s drug is than marketed hypertrophic cardiomyopathy medicines from Bristol Myers Squibb and Cytokinetics.

title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about Medicare and obesity drugs, Germany’s pricing plans, and more
summary: Weight loss drugs will be available through Medicare starting next month, thanks to a program that is supposed to be temporary but may be difficult to end

title: Neumora depression drug fails; EnGene to cut 50% of staff
summary: Neumora’s stumble is the latest setback for a once-promising class of brain drugs. Elsewhere, a cell therapy manufacturing specialist took steps toward an IPO and J&J made its latest U.S investment.

title: How supplement use changed over decades
summary: Medicare-funded weight loss, Utah's lower drunk driving limit, and more health news from Morning Rounds

summary: As the saying goes, one man’s trash is another man’s treasure. Or as Verge Labs might put it, one company’s failed clinical trial … is that same company’s new AI…

title: [Podcast] (Season 2) The Progress Profile: Alzheimer’s Research in Focus
summary: This podcast explores how leading Alzheimer’s experts are working to bridge the gap between clinical trial design and frontline care.

title: STAT+: Human Cell Atlas leader’s tie to 10x Genomics raises conflict-of-interest questions
summary: As the Human Cell Atlas project marks its 10th anniversary, a leader's ties to a major single-cell RNA sequencing company raise conflict-of-interest questions.

summary: America’s next health emergency won’t wait for Washington, write W. Craig Vanderwagen and Jennifer B. Alton.

summary: “Alzheimer’s is not primarily a disease of old age. It is a decades-long biological process,” writes Elizabeth Bevins.

title: STAT+: FDA approves Colorado’s plan to import cheaper drugs from Canada
summary: The FDA said it will allow Colorado to import certain prescription drugs from Canada in an effort to bring prices down for residents.

title: STAT+: Trump’s obesity drug plan creates a temporary Medicare program that may be hard to end
summary: After July 1, Medicare will offer GLP-1 weight-loss drugs for $50. It's supposed to be temporary, but chances are it won't be.

title: STAT+: A key European clinical trial registry lacks complete and timely results, an analysis finds
summary: A new analysis found that results for less than half of the clinical studies registered in a key European database were reported within the required time frame.

title: Covid vaccination cut risk of adverse heart events, large study finds
summary: Covid vaccination was also tied to a nearly 24% reduction in all-cause cardiac events in a large study, surprising researchers.

title: Medicare drug price rule may target under-the-skin Keytruda and Opdivo
summary: For 2029, a federal rule proposes closing a "loophole" that protects medicines transitioning from intravenous administration from price protection.

title: Lilly scores early positive data for new JAK drug
summary: Acquired through its purchase of Ajax, the drug showed encouraging effects in patients with hard-to-treat myelofibrosis. To analysts, such results could lead to competition for Incyte’s Jakafi.

title: J&J multiple myeloma drug gains edge in earlier stage disease
summary: The latest results studying Talvey in combination with Darzalex Faspro support J&J’s efforts to expand the immunotherapy's use.

summary: Jesse Gabriel "has passed more food policy in three years than most legislators enact in their entire careers," says one supporter.
